calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-06-2020 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant City And County Of San Franciscos Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-07-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Cody Christiansen, Esq. 510-646-9616 of Harris Personal Injury Lawyers for Plaintiff through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Off calendar as moot. The City was dismissed on December 6, 2019.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Defense Costs Pursuant To Ccp Section 1038
Off Calendar Dec-09-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Cody M. Christiansen, Esq. of HARRIS PERSONAL INJURY LAWYERS, T:510.646.9616, for plaintiff, on Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT CITY OF BRISBANE's Motion For Defense Costs Pursuant To CCP Section 1038, as follows:

Defendant City of Brisbane's motion for defense costs pursuant to CCP 1038 is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. The City of Brisbane has shown that it is entitled to defense costs from plaintiff Andrew Herman in the amount of $12,505.97.

Order and Judgment were signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-20-2019 Set For Trial On Mar-09-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Joinder
Off Calendar Oct-24-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Cody Christiansen, Esq. 510-646-9616 of Harris Personal Injury Lawyers for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Dawn Ceizler, Esq. 925-932-8225 for Defendant CITY OF BRISBANE.

RULING - The matter is Uncotested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's joinder to Brisbane's motion for summary judgment is denied. The City failed to provide sufficient notice per Code of Civ. Proc., 437c(a)(2) and neglected to file a separate statement of undisputed facts. (See Village Nurseries, L.P. v. Greenbaum (2002) 101 Cal.App.4th 26, 46-47 [joinder to motion for summary judgment cannot be granted where joining party fails to file its own separate statement of undisputed facts]; Frazee v. Seely (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 627, 636 [same].) In view of the Court's ruling on the City of Brisbane's motion, however, which raises identical issues, Plaintiff would be well-advised to stipulate to judgment in favor of the City and County of San Francisco, or to dismiss the complaint as to it.

Counsel for plaintiff is required to submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-24-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Cody Christiansen, Esq. 510-646-9616 of Harris Personal Injury Lawyers for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Dawn Ceizler, Esq. 925-932-8225 for Defendant CITY OF BRISBANE.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. Plaintiff argues. Defendant responds. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY OF BRISBANE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling in full. Defendant City of Brisbane's motion for summary judgment is granted.

The injury occurred on a paved bike path in the Candlestick Point Recreation State Area. (Complaint, 9-11, 14; Ceizler Decl., Ex. G.) The recreational path is part of the Bay Trail. (Breault Dec., 9-11.) Plaintiff's claim fails because Brisbane is entitled to trail immunity under Gov't Code 831.4(b). The undisputed evidence shows that the injury occurred because a condition of the trail, i.e., a gap in the concrete, caused plaintiff to fall while he was using the trail to access a recreational or scenic area. (See pictures attached to Plaintiff's Exs. B and D.) Under the circumstances, the City is entitled to absolute immunity under section 831.4(b). (See, e.g., Buergueno v. Regents of University of California (2015) 243 Cal.App.4th 1052 [affirming summary judgment for bicycle injury on paved bikeway]; Farnham v. City of Los Angeles (1998) 68 Cal.App.4th1097 [granting judgment on the pleadings based on trail immunity where portion of paved bikeway gave way and caused plaintiff to be thrown into ditch]; Carroll v. County of Los Angles (1997) 60 Cal.App.4th 606 [trail immunity applied to paved bike path with a crack that caused rollerblader to be injured]; Armenio v. County of San Mateo (1994) 28 Cal.App.4th 413 [trail immunity barred biker's claim where improper patching of the paved trail caused the injury].)

Plaintiff's reliance on Toeppe v. City of San Diego (2017) 13 Cal.App.5th 921 is misplaced. There, the court held that trail immunity did not apply where a branch from a eucalyptus tree that was a man-made addition fell on plaintiff. The court explained that the tree constituted a dangerous condition regardless of the location of the subject trail. "In short, this is not a case about trails. It is about trees. Trees that were planted and maintained by the City. Trees that were not naturally occurring in Mission Bay Park." (Toeppe, 13 Cal.App.5th at 931.) Here, plaintiff is seeking to recover based on the condition of the trail, which included the utility box. The dangerous condition (gap in the pavement) cannot be divorced from the trail and therefore, Toeppe is inapposite.

The Court denies the City of Brisbane's request for an award of attorneys' fees per Code of Civ. Proc., 1038. Brisbane has not presented any evidence of any costs or attorneys' fees it incurred.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
